prose:
Method-provenance block
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), f⁰ (radical) and dual descriptor for ferrocene; identification of the most electrophilic (largest f⁺) and most nucleophilic (largest f⁻) atoms.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) implemented in xtb 6.x.
Geometry provenance: Fixed input Cartesian coordinates supplied by user (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz”).  No geometry optimisation was performed; the calculation is a single-point evaluation on that geometry (fukui-reactivity//GFN2-xTB).
Solvent / environment: Gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge and multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet parent).  Cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states were treated as doublets (multiplicity 2) with charges +1 and –1, respectively, as per default scheme.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand “fukui”) calling xTB 6.x.
Convergence & warnings: All three single-point calculations (N, N–1, N+1) converged.  Integrity status ok; engine reported three warnings (verbatim below).  Live SCF/out-of-core log was written during the run to a file named “fukui_<timestamp>.out” in the working directory (path was displayed at launch and can be tailed for full details).

Key results
• Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺):  Fe (atom 11), f⁺ = 0.22885456
• Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻):   Fe (atom 11), f⁻ = 0.25938191
• Dual descriptor on Fe: –0.030527354 (negative ⇒ nucleophilic character dominates)
